Jon: 	Okay, welcome to the video tutorial on the bigger solution, this is
actually step 4 of the VSL process, this is step 2 of the last few video
sections we’ve gone over.
And this section of your VSL is where you can deliver the most value-
heavy content possible to your prospect. This is where the tips come
into place. And remember, you’ve done the 3-tip loop before. You’ve
already done the 3 tips. So, if you haven’t, refer back to that video
assignment in the 1-2-3 formula section. I believe that’s video #4.
Now, this is going to conclude with the simple transition into the final
step of the grand offer. The following are example slides of how I did
this for one of my products, and I want to walk through this from a
wordsmithing point of view.
I’m opening a loop here in step one. “I’ll go into specifics on the caloric
staggering principle in just a second.”
Now, if you remember back from the previous video, I started this loop
by saying, “And the most important tip of all is my caloric staggering
principle.”
In other words, I opened the loop with my primary benefit’s driving
force. “What makes my primary benefit work? It’s called caloric
staggering principle.” I coined a term. We highly advise that you coin
a term yourself.
So, “I’ll go into specifics on the caloric staggering principle,” which I’ve
already told them is the most important, “In just a second. So, by all
means, keep watching.” This is forward momentum.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

2
Now, your avoid tip comes in here first, and your avoid tip details will
go here as many slides as you see fit.
Now, here’s how I moved into my avoid tip, and the exact copy that I
used. “First,” before we get into all of this, in other words, “First, you
absolutely need to know about the one food you must avoid, if you
want to lose weight. Actually, this is going to surprise you. Most
people believe this food is healthy, and it can be, just never when most
people choose to eat it.”
Look what I’m doing here. All I’m doing is building curiosity up for the
tip itself. If you wanted to go right into the tip, that would be fine. I’m
just giving you some wordsmithing techniques to move into those tips.
So, in other words, I won’t tell them what the tip is right off the bat. I’ll
say, “First, I’ve got to let you in on a tip,” and I’ll tell them the reason
the tip is cool. This was all done in the tip formula. “Why is it cool?
Well, it’s because most people don’t understand this and most people
think that this food is healthy. They’re just eating it at the wrong time.”
So, I’m telling them it is healthy, but never when most people choose
to eat it. So then, I continue, “And if you eat it, you’ll shut down your
fat-burning hormones for hours.”
So, at this point, everybody’s kind of curious what this is.
“I want you to avoid starchy carbohydrates in the morning.” Now, the
only reason I emphasize the word “morning” here is because it’s a tip.
I’m not trying to subliminally indicate anything. Obviously, not morning.
I’m just wanting to draw their eye to the attention of the premise of the
tip. “I want people to avoid starchy carbohydrates in the morning.”
“And this is just until you get back on the weight loss track. It’s not
forever, so don’t worry.”
Notice what I’m doing here. If I have a tip for somebody, and I know
that it’s going to be a little difficult, like asking people to give up their
morning cereal, and if your industry is making money online and you’re
asking someone to spend an hour a day planning a budget or stuff that
you’re not going to want to do, then you want to always draw it back.
You always want to say, “Now, you don’t have to do this forever. This
is not a forever kind of thing. Just do this until you get to point X.”
And, in my case, point X is, “Until you get your weight loss on track.
It’s not forever, so don’t worry.”
“Simply,” and this is how to do it, this is all right out of the tip formula.
If you need a refresher course, go back and look at the videos on the
tip formula, “Simply replace those carbs with low-sugar berries or even
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

veggies. And doing this one tip will allow you to burn far more fat

under control, which can protect you from heart diseases and type 2
diabetes.”
Now, I walk them through. Remember, this is all out of the tip formula.
Walk them through their little mini dream story. “Just picture it. You
make this small change, and without sacrificing any ‘calories,’ you are
dropping fat like never before, feeling more energy. Isn’t that an
awesome thing to imagine, all from such a simple tip?”
I just walked through the tip formula for my avoid tip, step by step, slide
by slide. Again, the tip formula is found in the tip formula video. So,
go check that out if you don’t remember it. Hopefully, you have your 3
tips in front of you and you’ve already done this.
Now, notice what I’ve highlighted in this slide, because it’s pretty
clever. I’ve taken the liberty of highlighting 2 words, “awesome,”
“simple.” Leaps right out at you, doesn’t it? Awesome, simple.
Now, I want to implant words like that throughout my entire video sales
letter, which we’ve show you, so far, how to do. Awesome, simple.
Really, really great words to emphasis.
Now, I’m going to tell them what to do, “Feel free to eat carbs in the
morning, if you really want to, on your ‘feed days.’” I’m actually
building a little bit of a pattern interrupt here. And also, I’m opening
another loop. Because, “feed days,” notice I’ve got that in quotations.
“More about that, in just a moment.”
So, I am always, continually, throughout my video sales letter, saying,
“More about that in a minute. I’ll tell you in just a few seconds.” What
am I doing? I am forcing the user forward. I am prompting the user to
go forward, to continue watching. “More about that in just a moment.”
I’m going to move into my enjoy tip now. Now, the enjoy tips are going
to go here, as many slides as you see fit, and here are my specific
examples.
“Now, how would you like to actually enjoy eating foods that you know
will make you lose weight and feel incredible?” I set that up pretty
good didn’t I?
“If you take advantage,” notice the words “take advantage” are
highlighted, because I’m going to ask them to take advantage of my
offer later on, in the grand offer.

4
“If you take advantage of this next simple tip, you will,” and then I’ve
just put dot, dot, dot, because I just wanted to fill in with what would
happen here if they did that.
I want to also get to this main point here. So, I say, “You will feel
awesome. You will do this.” I’ll just list a series of benefits right there.
There’s no reason to go over every single slide.
But I pulled in this one, because this is an awesome place to put – in
any of your tips, by the way – an enjoy, avoid, and how to. You can
use this strategy in any of your tips. And that is pulling out a term, a
phrase that people don’t know. Those make awesome tips.
If you have a relationship product, and there’s a term called “love
languages,” and there is, maybe a lot of people don’t know that book, a
lot of people don’t know that.
Or there’s a term called “cognitive dissidence.” If you pull out those
kind of terms and people go, “Ooh, that sounds kind of funky,” that’s
good, that gets people’s attention.
So, I’m doing this here by saying, “You’ll also decrease a fat-storing
hormone called cortisol.” Now, what’s cortisol? I tease them. I don’t
tell them exactly what it is.
“All you need to do, is a simple exercise. Not a physical exercise,
rather, one that requires just a pen and paper. I want you to write
down 5 protein foods that you know are good for you, and that you
enjoy. Just 5, for now.”
What I’m doing here is I’m walking someone through a very simple
exercise to help them enjoy the foods that they’re going to be eating.
They don’t have to work on enjoying unhealthy foods, right? “Here’s
how you enjoy healthy foods,” and I give them some food examples. I
tell them, “Look through these foods, and basically write down 5 that
you really like.”
Now, I want them to write down 5 more foods, and these are
carbohydrate foods that you enjoy eating and that are great for weight
loss. I don’t expect them to know this, so I tell them what they are.
“Out of this list, apples, brown rice, pears, berries, quinoa.”
“Now, write down 3 essential fats. Olive oil, safflower oil, canola oil,
real butter, etc.”
“Once you get your list, the next task is super simple. Refuse to eat
anything else than the specific, healthy foods you love.” That’s my tip.
In other words, my tip sounds so simple, and I actually even say that

5
on the next few slides.
My tip is super simple; so simple, especially a nutritionist, would think
that, “Oh, that’s common sense, just eat foods you like.” It’s not, and
I’m about to tell them why this isn’t common sense, in just a few slides.
But I am just telling my user to only eat the healthy foods that they love
the most, and refuse to eat anything else. So the next slide says, “If
you hate broccoli, and you know broccoli is healthy for you, right? But
if you hate it, but if you enjoy bell pepper, toss the broccoli away and
enjoy your peppers. Just do that, just forget broccoli exists, right?”
“Now, this sounds very obvious.” Just what I was saying earlier, “It
sounds very obvious, but guess what?” Now, look at “guess what.”
“Guess what” is another form of an anchor drop. It’s a question, but it’s
like, “Well, guess what?” and you’re waiting for the answer. So, you’re
pushing the slide forward.
“Most people believe,” notice I don’t say “you believe,” “most people
believe that they have to eat foods that taste terrible to lose weight.”
See, this is the crux of my tip, my tip was, this is a way that you don’t
have to eat terrible-tasting foods, and the best way to do that is to eat
your favorite healthy foods.
“It sounds like so easy and so obvious, but this is exactly why it works,
because most people think they have to eat broccoli. For example, if
you hate broccoli, because it tastes awful. So, if it tastes awful, it must
be good for weight loss. And that’s a lie.”
Notice what I’m doing here? I am literally building a lie, just like we
talked about in the last video, inside a tip. See, this if very flexible
stuff, “Most people believe this, but you know what? That’s a lie.”
What am I doing? I’m just building a little miniature big problem, right
here, inside my bigger solution tip.
“Once you take charge, your diet becomes a nutrition plan, your
personal handbook to weight loss success,” I’m future-pacing them.
“Imagine this.” Now, I’m going to give them a dream story. “Imagine
this: never having to eat anything except foods that taste good to you.”
Now, that’s all I said. I didn’t walk them through anything further than
that, because it wasn’t necessary. That was it, “Imagine never having
to eat anything unless it tastes good to you.”
Now, I segue into the final tip, which is the how-to.
So, I’m going to close that loop that I opened at the very first of this

segment, where I say, “I’m going to tell you more about that caloric

So, you’re going to do the same thing. “I’m going to tell you more

Now, this goes on for as many slides as you see fit, and here’s how I
did it. I did it like this, and this is a great technique to use. “And it gets
even better.”
See, the enjoy tips are perfect for setting up the how-to tips, because
enjoy tips are obviously very positive. This is going to make somebody
feel good.
So, after they already feel good, a great way to go into your how-to is
“It gets even better. It gets even better.” So, again, I highlighted that
in red.
Here comes the really big, massive, crucial tip. Forget grammar here,
folks. Forget grammar. Don’t worry about being grammatically
correct, worry about making sales. Worry about people clicking on that
buy button. That’s what you need to be focused on, and this takes the
worry out of it.
Here comes the really big, massive, crucial tip. “Warning, without
using tip #3, you can forget about those other tips helping you lose
weight.”
if you’ll go back and look at the tip formula, we talk about taking the
how-to tip and making it the most crucial tip, because it’s going to lead
into your product, the grand offer.
But here’s where you want to put a warning up, and you want to say, “If
you don’t do this one tip, you can forget about all those other tips.” So
they’re thinking, “Wow, those other tips were great, so I’ve got to do
this tip.”
Well, that’s what’s really powerful about this. When you say you have
to do this tip, and you anchor that tip to your product, what do they
have to do? They have to get your product. Otherwise, they feel like,
“Oh, well, those other tips where good, but this is really the crux of it
all.” This is what you want to build.
“Warning!” That word “warning” just sticks out like a sore thumb,
because I’ve highlighted it in red, that’s my subliminal. There’s nothing
subliminal about warning, it’s just a big red warning at you. “You can
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

7
forget about the other tips helping you unless you do this. This is the
tip that will allow you to eat whatever foods you love and crave.”
Notice I say love and crave? Because most people, in my product
field, they crave unhealthy foods. So, “You can eat those foods, all
those cheat meal foods,” look, I’ve coined another term, cheat meal,
“throughout the week, while dropping all the weight you desire.”
If you look back over my previous videos, you’ll notice that I use the
word “desire” a lot. It’s one of my favorite power words, because
people don’t want things, they desire things.
We use the word “want,” and it’s such a trite word. “Desire” is not trite.
“Desire” is super powerful.
So, when you talk to the people that are your prospects, you want to
get to their desires. And remember, we are subliminally and now
literally moving people out of decision mode, and into what? Desire
mode. Out of decision mode, into desire mode. And one of the most
efficient ways to do that is to mention the word desire over and over
and over again.
Now you can see the psychology behind what we’ve been talking
about through the entire segment of videos that came before this.
“This is what you want to do, while dropping all the weight that you
desire.” And I keep going.
“This tip actually forces you to consume high-calorie, tasty foods
throughout the week. Get this…”
Now, why do you think I made “get this” red? It’s subliminal, right?
“Get this.” “Get this” is obviously a double entendre. I want people to
get this product. They don’t even know it’s a product yet, by the way.
In your VSL, no one knows, at this point, that you’re selling anything.
People might suspect it, granted, but a lot of people won’t have a clue.
So, when you start saying “get this,” “buy into this,” those are the
phrases that really, really start setting the neurological patterns up to
click that buy button.
“So, these tips force you.” The “forces you” is very powerful. It’s
almost negatively powerful. But in this case, it’s awesome. “It’s
literally forcing you, twisting your arm.” You can say something like
that. Remember, we can use comparisons. That’s not a true
comparison, but it’s something that people have heard a phrase all
their life, “I might have to twist your arm to get you to eat your favorite
foods, those high calorie sweets.” It’s kind of a joke, right?
“But get this, in order to lose weight.” So, I’m building this tip up

See how I just read over the words? I embellished. They don’t know

9
where I’m going to start or stop.
“When you decrease calories for more than 48 to 72 hours, these
hormones take a nose dive. However, decreasing calories for a very
short period of time has been shown to increase weight loss, and
increase the speed at which you lose weight.”
Now, look at what I’ve done here with the highlights. Short, speed.
People love, when they’re looking at a diet, they love a diet to be short
and they love it to work fast. That’s why those words are highlighted.
“So, my system,” this is the first hint, right here, the first hint that there
is actually a system, “my system takes advantage of both worlds, the
low calorie, healthy food days, followed at a specific time…” Now,
notice what I’m doing here. At this point, I am opening a loop that I will
never close, until they get to my actual product. I will never close this
loop. In other words, I’m opening a permanent loop, and the only way
they can get the answer to it is to buy my product.
This is what you want to do during your how-to tip. You want to open a
loop that the reader cannot close.
That’s the most preferable way to do that. So, you want to tell them
that. For example, if you want to make money online and you have a
technique for doing that, “If you use a certain search engine, or if you
use a certain protocol,” if it’s a paper shredding company, let’s say.
You give them a tip about keeping the document secure or whatever
you want to say, that, “We shred, and we’re certified, so we can make
sure that you’re super-secure, if you know what to look for, or if our
certificate has a certain set of numbers on it.” It could be something
like that.
So, when you get into the services and when you get into the actual
grand offer, then you can kind of let them off the hook and let them
know what that is.
But for this particular tip, I know that this is the crux of my entire book.
Remember, this is my primary benefit we’re talking about here, so I’m
not going to tell them everything. I’m just going to tell them some
important stuff, and leave out the details. We went through this in the
tip formula.
So, I’m leaving out the details, and I start getting really serious about
this. I say, “This is vital. It cannot be whatever time you want, it has to
be a specific time.” So, right now, people are going, “Oh. Okay,
when?”
“Follow it at a specific time, with your favorite foods. And the result?” I

don’t tell them when. “Your T4 never decreases, and that’s awesome,
because T4 decreasing is exactly why all diets stop working. Yet
you’ve tricked your body into burning off mounds of unwanted weight.”
Now, notice what I’ve done here, wordsmithing-wise.
“Yet,” instead of “but,” “you’ve tricked your body” – it’s tricked, tricked is
a very powerful word – “into burning off mounds.” I didn’t say pounds, I
said mounds. “Mounds of unwanted weight, without breaking the T4
boundary, plus,” anchor drop, “increasing calories, again, only at
specific time, reboots that other hormone, leptin, and leptin is your fat-
burning pal, let me tell you.”
“Increasing leptin through strategic over-feeding…” I’ve coined
another phrase here that I’m tossing in. It’s really the same thing, by
the way, caloric staggering, strategic over-feeding, “…at specific times
during the week, gives you the freedom no other diet plan can offer

What do people want more than anything? Freedom. Love, freedom,
those are the big things, right? So, I emphasis freedom and love
whenever possibly. This is what they really want, freedom. “You

“You actually use your favorite foods as a tool to burn fat and keep

Now, remember, this is the imagine this dream sequence, mini dream

Now, I want you to watch specifically how I go through this section.
This is important. “Just picture it for yourself. Here you are…” I want
to stop right there. When I say, “Here you are,” that is as present-
tense, present-location as you can get. I’m using vernacular. “Here
you are.” We say it all the time. “Here you are doing fill-in-the-blank.”
“Here you are” pulls someone right into the present moment. Even
right into your general vicinity. So, obviously, they can’t be thinking,
“Maybe this will be me,” or whatever. So I say, “Here you are, eating
your favorite healthy low-carb meals, burning fat, feeling awesome
about yourself.” Again, it’s all about feeling. “Feeling awesome about
yourself, when all of a sudden it’s pizza time. Or whatever food you
desire.”
If you go back over the tip formula, you’ll see that we never get

11
specific. I’ll say “pizza time,” because it sounds good. And then I’ll say
“or whatever food you desire.” So that opens up, somebody hates
pizza, they like burgers? “Oh, great than I can eat burgers.”
So, “When all of a sudden, it’s pizza time. Without a shred of guilt or
feeling as if you broke your diet, you call up a few friends.” Notice how
all this is present tense. “…You call up a few friends, and you ask
them to meet you down at your favorite food joint, and they marvel at
you.”
Marvel is an awesome power word. Write this down. Rather than
“amazed” and all that, “marvel” is awesome. “And they marvel at you.”
And then, I put a quote from their friends in here. “How on earth can
you be losing that much weight and be eating this food, too, the junk
food, the pizza, the burgers?” And this is the clincher, “This food used
to make you fat.” “What’s up? How are you doing this?”
What I’ve done is I’ve future-paced them. Notice I don’t ever say that
they’re skinny, that they’ve dropped 40 pounds. I just future-pace them
through it.
So, all I do is I walk them through. I say, “You go, without a shred of
guilt, you call up your friends, you say, ‘Meet me at my favorite food
joint,’” and all I say is “They marvel. “They’re the ones testifying to
your weight loss. ‘How on earth can you be losing that much weight?’”
I never said that. Their friends did. In other words, I have built in, in a
very sneaky way, social proof. This is social proof in an imaginary
setting. I’m having their friends complement them, rather than me tell
them they’re losing weight.
This is one of the most ninja copywriting tricks you can ever steal, so
steal it. “How on earth can you be losing that much weight?” This
could work for anything. Someone can say, “How on earth is your
company so secure?” “How on earth are your children protected from
the web?”
For someone that might sell like a digital security product. “How on
earth or your children protected from the web when all these other kids
are struggling with this? How is that possible, when they surf the web
more than my kids?”
This works for anything. “How on earth can you losing that much
weight and eating this food too? This is the food that used to make
you fat! What’s up?” It’s a great question, isn’t it?
Well, that leads them into the intro. The transition, I’m going to get out

of the tip here.
“Here’s what’s up. You now know the formula.” Remember the words

Now, this is where I transition into the grand offer, so those were my

So, at this time, I’ve gone through the avoid tip, the enjoy tip, and the

Step 5 of the 5 step VSL process, the grand offer. And by far and
away, the most important step. It’s absolutely critical that you get the
grand offer right.
Now, your transition will follow a simple formula. Step one, you have 2
choices. Step 2, take the easier path. Step 3, I’ve done the work for
you. Step 4, and it’s call.
Now, if you are a service industry, these may not apply to you. May
not apply to you. Most people can work around that. You really only
have to use step one and step 2, if you’re in a service industry.
In other words, you can’t say, “I’ve done the work for you,” if you’re
selling some service. If it’s a bed and breakfast that you’re doing this,
you can’t really say, “I’ve done the work for you,” because no one is
wanting to build a bed and breakfast, probably. But you can definitely
take the 2 choices route. And I’ll show you how all this works in
specific examples now.
I’ll say, “That was a lot to cover, and you may be feeling a bit
overwhelmed or confused, and that’s okay.” So, what am I doing
here? I’ve given them so much information in the video, and without
saying, “You know what? I’ve given you so much value-packed
information in this video.” I’m not having to say it, I just said, “That was
a lot to cover, and you may be feeling overwhelmed.”
I am more focused on what they’re feeling than on what I’m giving
them. Do you see the distinction there? Most marketers take the
approach of, “Wow, how awesome was my product? How awesome
was this? Isn’t this incredible?”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 15 --

13
I’m more interested in how they’re feeling. And I know that after giving
them a lot of information and big words like cortisol and strategic eating
and all that, caloric staggering, that they may be feeling overwhelmed
and confused. And I tell them it’s okay. It’s completely okay.
The bottom line is I kind of want them to feel a little overwhelmed and
confused. And here’s why, “Basically, this means you have to choices
on where you go from here.” So, I’ve set this up: “2 choices, choice
one, take the information in this presentation and try to go it alone.
Just try to do this yourself. Who knows, you may see some success, if
you took really careful notes.”
Notice what I’m doing here. It’s almost tongue-in-cheek. “You may
figure it out. You may get lucky,” is what I’m saying, because I gave
you a lot of great information. You may have to watch this video 6
times to get it all down, and you’ll probably not get that whole “you
have to eat it at a specific time” thing, because I didn’t tell you.”
But I’m not telling them that here. All I say is, “Who knows, you may
see some success, if you took careful notes. Or there’s the choice 2,
the savvy choice.” Here’s where you want to use word like “smart,”
“intelligent.” I like savvy. That’s just one of my favorite words. You
can use whatever word you want.
The smart choice, the savvy choice, there’s choice 2. So, what have I
set them up already to pick? I’ve already set them up to pick choice 2,
right? Because it’s the savvy choice, and no one wants to be not
savvy. So, there’s the savvy choice.
“This is the quickest, easiest, and smartest way to get the body and
health you deserve.” I start with “this.” What do I mean by “this?” The
savvy choice. And I also mean the product that I’m about to introduce
to them. So, that’s what I mean by this.
“This, what you’re about to see, is the quickest easiest and smartest
way to get the body and health you deserve. So, let me do the work
for you.” Sorry about the red typo there. That f means nothing. I
promise, that’s just a typo. Yes, subliminal typo, F.
“Let me do the work for you. And guess what, you know what? I
already have, I’ve already done the work for you.” This is step 3, “I’ve
done the work for you.” That’s really all you have to say. All you have
to say in this slide is, “You know what? I’ve done the work for you.
Why start from scratch and try to reinvent the wheel?”
This is the whole pattern behind the I’ve done it for you. “Why struggle
through this? Why try to figure it out on your own, when I’ve laid out
your exact step-by-step blueprint for weight loss success. I’ve already
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 15 --

14
done it. I’ve already done it for you.”
This is the big, big sales positioning secret. What you want to position
your product or service as is something that has done the work for the
person, so they can enjoy the benefits. No one wants to work at
something; they want to enjoy the benefits.
Now, that does not mean that if you’re selling a product on how to do
something, or a product like 3X VSL, that you don’t have to work at it.
You should lie to somebody. But if I’ve already done the blueprint for
you, which I have, I’ve shown you exactly, step by step, how to do this,
why would you want to ever go out and try to do it on your own? I’ve
already proven that it works.
And this is where you really want to twist it, a little bit. “One that’s been
tested for years,” assuming that yours has, “one that’s responsible for
countless thousands of men and women of all ages losing the weight.”
So, I’ve really made this compelling. “Why reinvent the wheel when
you can have a formula that’s been tested, it’s been proven by
thousands, and getting that dream body at last,” I’m just walking them
through a mini dream story, “without suffering, without starvation, and
without cramping your lifestyle?”
You may notice what I did here, as well. I used the without technique.
See, you can use the 3X VSL tool box techniques throughout your
entire video sales letter, slide by slide, wherever you want. There’s not
a slide-specific thing to teach you. Here, you don’t do it on this slide
and not on another slide. You can use them just about anywhere.
So, I used the 3 withouts. “You can get that dream body at last,” and
notice where I say at last, at last, it’s finally here, “without suffering,
without starvation, and without cramping your lifestyle.” Those are 3
huge withouts for me.
Now, I conclude this with the step called “and it’s called.” In other
words, I’m just going to introduce the product here. I’m going to
introduce the product. “Here’s what the vast majority decide to do. It’s
called…,” and that’s it.
You could say, “And it’s called,” that could be the entire slide, but I
decided to make it a little more wordsmithy, as my favorite word.
“Here’s what the vast majority decide to do.” What have I done here?
Inclusion statement. I’ve included the vast majority. People want to be
a part of the vast majority. The vast majority. “This is what the vast
majority pick.”
That’s why when you see certain checkout places, like when you go to
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 14 of 15 --

15
checkout at amazon.com, they’ll say, “The majority of people that
bought this, also bought this,” or they’ll say, “A lot of people who
bought this, also bought this.”
And the reason that works is simple: it’s inclusion, and it’s also social
proof.
So, I’m building in social proof again, without saying anything, not a
single testimonial. The testimonials come in the grand offer.
I am building in subconscious and conscious social proof by saying,
“The vast majority have chosen option 2. They’ve decided to do this.
And option 2 is called…,” and that is the end. That is the end of this
segment.
So, you will go from here into the grand offer. That is the transition into
the grand offer. So when you start hitting the grand offer is when you
introduce the product itself. That is the sequence to build up through
the bigger solution. You’ve already done the majority of the work here,
and now just follow the wordsmithing techniques I’ve laid out in this
video, and you should knock it out of the park.
